title: Nutty Creamy Mung Beans Spanish Stir-fry
description: A nostalgic dish featuring mung bean sprouts and toasted nuts for a creamy and nutty texture in a simple yet flavorful Spanish-inspired stir-fry, egg-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup of heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Or substitute with coconut milk for a vegan option

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: To add flavor without adding oil or dairy

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing and tossing the ingredients together

instructions: Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat and add the vegetable oil.
Add mung bean sprouts to the hot pan, stirring occasionally until they are just starting to wilt.
Toss in the toasted almond pieces and garlic for about 30 seconds, allowing them to release their oils and aromas.
Pour in the lemon juice and stir well to incorporate all flavors evenly.
If using red pepper flakes, add them now and mix thoroughly. The goal is to let the heat develop but not overcook as they can burn quickly.
Slowly pour in the heavy cream (or coconut milk for vegan version), stirring constantly to ensure everything melts together smoothly and does not curdle.
Reduce the heat if necessary to prevent burning, and cook until the sauce has slightly thickened. This should take about 2-3 minutes more at low-medium heat.
Turn off the heat immediately once the dish reaches your desired level of creaminess. Serve hot with additional garnishes like chopped fresh herbs for a festive touch.
